title: Phase Baseline Audit Remediation (R036) — Clean Baseline Before Next Phase
scope:
One-time remediation of the four-stream baseline audit (R036) conducted at the close of PLA-0006. Brings the codebase to a measurable clean baseline before the next phase begins: go vet clean, npx tsc --noEmit clean, all four custom lints clean, zero inline style={{...}} hard-rule violations, accurate schema_migrations bookkeeping, all forward migrations correctly wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT, no dead exported Go functions, no unwired env-vars, no untracked migration files, and a re-migrated test DB tier so backend tests can run green again.

Out of scope: package major-version upgrades (Next.js 15→16, React 18→19, TypeScript 5→6) and the 233-index idx_scan = 0 drop. Both are captured as tech-debt entries with one-sprint observation windows; neither belongs in this clean-up wave.
value:
The next phase needs a stable signal floor. Today we cannot trust go vet (it fails on a stale test), the test pool DB is missing recent RBAC migrations (tests fail with column does not exist), the CSS hard rule is silently violated 119 times, and 3 hidden fetch() calls bypass the silent-refresh path. Each of these will compound during the next phase if not closed now.

Closing this plan delivers seven measurable signals: (1) go vet ./... exits 0, (2) npx tsc --noEmit exits 0, (3) all four lints exit 0, (4) zero inline style={{...}} matches via grep, (5) schema_migrations matches the filesystem on both DBs, (6) every forward migration ends with an explicit COMMIT;, and (7) cd backend && go test ./... runs zero column does not exist errors against the migrated test DB. After landing, every later phase starts from these seven signals as the baseline.
implementation_plan: Wave 1 (build unblock, 1 story): fix the portfoliomodels/list_test.go DTO drift so go vet ./... exits 0. Single story, contained edit, must land first because every other backend story depends on a clean vet baseline.

Wave 2 (cleanup & hygiene, 8 stories, all LOW risk): mechanical sweep — delete the residual schema_migrations DOWN row, append missing COMMITs to 4 migrations, document the 027 numbering gap, create symmetric library/down/ folder, delete 5 unused exported Go functions, audit OLLAMA_URL/OLLAMA_MODEL env-vars, npm update marked patch bump, commit untracked migration + DOWN README. Each story is independently mergeable; none touch live request paths.

Wave 3 (drift closures, 5 stories, mostly MED risk): the contract-touching work — migrate 3 direct fetch() calls onto api(); sweep inline-style violations to zero; reset test DB tier to migration tip; add make test-db-reset target with doc; capture 233-index audit as tech-debt entry with observation window. These need careful testing because they touch live UI / tests / runtime paths.
Closure: re-run go vet, tsc, and all 4 lints across the working tree; confirm the seven baseline signals; mark PLA-0011 complete in the plan registry.
areas_impacted: DEV: portfoliomodels test fix, dead-export deletes, OLLAMA env audit, library/down/ folder, untracked-file commit, test DB reset, make test-db-reset target
SQL: schema_migrations DOWN-row cleanup, COMMIT append on 4 migrations, 233-index tech-debt entry
UI: marked patch bump, inline-style sweep across app/ and dev/
API: 3 direct fetch() → api() helper migrations (DomRegistryContext, Panel, Header)
Docs: c_c_postgresql_migrations.md (027 gap + DOWN row note), c_postgresql.md (test DB tier doc), c_tech_debt.md (233-index S3 entry)

schema_migrations matches filesystem on mmff_vector and mmff_library
All 101 vector + 11 library forward migrations end with explicit COMMIT;
Numbering gap at db/schema/027 documented with git-log reference
db/library_schema/down/ exists with README symmetric to db/schema/down/
Five unused exported Go functions (ValidateName, ValidateKind, 2× Parse*, IsPAdmin, ParseEntityKey) deleted
OLLAMA_URL/OLLAMA_MODEL either wired in .env.dev with documented defaults or removed from main.go
Three direct fetch() calls migrated onto api() helper (DomRegistryContext, Panel, Header)
Zero inline style={{...}} violations across app/ and dev/
Test DB at port 5434 migrated to current tip; backend test suite runs zero column-does-not-exist errors
make test-db-reset target idempotently rebuilds the test DB
233 unused indexes captured as Severity-3 tech-debt entry with observation window end date
features_removed: Five unused exported Go functions in addressables, artefacts, nav packages
Direct fetch() pattern outside api()/SSE in app/contexts and app/components
Inline style={{...}} hard-rule violations across app/ and dev/

risks.risk: Inline-style sweep (00393) introduces visual regressions when migrating dynamic values into CSS custom properties.
risks.mitigation: Manual smoke of /topology, /work-items, /theme included in the AC. Land 00393 in its own PR so any regression has a single revert target.

risks.risk: Test DB reset (00394) destroys the local test pool, causing incidental test flakiness for whoever runs tests during the cutover window.
risks.mitigation: Land alongside 00395 (the make test-db-reset target) so the recovery path is documented and one command. Announce in the dev channel before cutover.

risks.risk: fetch()→api() migration (00392) changes auth-error semantics on three call sites; a previously-silent 401 may now trigger a refresh + retry that exposes a different failure mode.
risks.mitigation: AC requires a stale-token reproduction proving the addressables snapshot still loads. Roll into staging first if available.

risks.risk: OLLAMA env-var deletion (00389) removes a hidden integration point that may be re-needed when the LLM feature lights up.
risks.mitigation: If the env-vars are removed rather than wired, capture the decision and the original line numbers in the same commit message so future re-introduction is traceable.

risks.risk: Migration COMMIT appends (00385) re-touch already-applied migration files; if anyone runs them again they should be no-ops, but a careless re-apply could surface unexpected side effects.
risks.mitigation: All four files have already been applied on dev; the runner records them by filename and refuses to re-run. AC explicitly checks 'up to date' on dry-run.

references.label: R036 — Phase Baseline Audit research paper
